title: Naming Request
description: The public request contract used to ask for a resource to be named, tagged, or labeled according to project conventions. Callers supply only the information specific to the resource being requested; the Context Resolution pipeline enriches this request into a complete Resource Identity and Governance Context (see resource-identity.schema.json and governance-context.schema.json). This schema is intentionally small and is expected to grow in future versions.

convention.description: Identifier of the Convention Pack used to resolve and evaluate the request.

resource_type.description: The type of resource being requested.

functional.description: Functional Identity information specific to this request.

service.description: Logical workload, subsystem, or deployable capability within the system.

component.description: Optional functional role or subcomponent within the service.

deployment.description: Deployment Identity information specific to this request. Most deployment information is resolved by Context Providers rather than supplied here.

instance.description: Distinguishes multiple instances of an otherwise identical resource.

overrides.description: Explicit values that intentionally bypass attributes otherwise resolved by Context Resolution. Overrides mirror the canonical Resource Identity and Governance Context hierarchy rather than arbitrary key/value pairs. They are still validated by the Convention Engine and do not bypass Resource Definition constraints, Convention Pack restrictions, or schema validation.

custom_metadata.description: Additional caller-supplied metadata that does not map to a defined Resource Identity attribute.
